name: Ibuprofen
dosage: Adults: 200-400 mg every 4-6 hours. Max OTC dose: 1200 mg/day. Prescription max: 2400 mg/day.
sideEffects: Increased risk of GI bleeding with long-term use
contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to NSAIDs or aspirin
warnings: Take with food to minimize GI side effects. Avoid prolonged use without medical supervision. Caution in elderly patients.

name: Paracetamol (Acetaminophen)
dosage: Adults: 500-1000 mg every 4-6 hours. Max: 4 g/day (3 g/day in elderly or hepatic impairment).
sideEffects: Generally well tolerated at therapeutic doses
interactions: Warfarin (chronic high doses may potentiate effect)
Enzyme-inducing drugs (carbamazepine, rifampicin) increase hepatotoxicity risk
warnings: Overdose can cause severe liver damage. Do not exceed maximum daily dose. Avoid alcohol during treatment.

name: Amoxicillin
dosage: Adults: 250-500 mg every 8 hours, or 500-875 mg every 12 hours, depending on indication.
contraindications: History of severe immediate hypersensitivity reaction to a beta-lactam
warnings: Complete the full prescribed course even if symptoms improve. Inform prescriber of any penicillin allergy.

name: Metformin
dosage: Start 500 mg once or twice daily with meals; titrate up to a max of 2000-3000 mg/day in divided doses.
sideEffects: Vitamin B12 deficiency with long-term use
interactions: Iodinated contrast media (temporary discontinuation may be required)
warnings: Take with food. Monitor renal function periodically. Pause before procedures involving IV contrast.

name: Lisinopril
dosage: Adults: 10-20 mg once daily; titrate up to a max of 40 mg/day.
sideEffects: Acute kidney injury in susceptible patients
warnings: Monitor renal function and serum potassium. Discontinue immediately if angioedema occurs.

name: Omeprazole
dosage: Adults: 20-40 mg once daily, ideally 30 minutes before breakfast.
sideEffects: Long-term: increased risk of fractures, hypomagnesemia, B12 deficiency, C. difficile infection
contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to omeprazole or other PPIs
Concomitant use with rilpivirine or nelfinavir
warnings: Reassess need for long-term therapy regularly. Use the lowest effective dose for the shortest duration.

name: Salbutamol (Albuterol)
dosage: Inhaler: 1-2 puffs (100 mcg/puff) as needed, max 8 puffs/24 h. Nebulizer: 2.5-5 mg up to four times daily.
contraindications: Caution in severe cardiovascular disease and hyperthyroidism
warnings: Increasing frequency of use suggests worsening asthma control - reassess therapy. Not a replacement for long-term controller medication.

name: Cetirizine
dosage: Adults and children > 12 y: 10 mg once daily. Reduce dose in renal impairment.
contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to cetirizine, levocetirizine, or hydroxyzine
interactions: Alcohol and other CNS depressants (additive sedation)
Theophylline (slightly reduced cetirizine clearance at high doses)
warnings: May cause drowsiness - caution when driving or operating machinery. Adjust dose in renal impairment.
